title: Add Collections to Adaptive Orbit Dock (4th item) with Collections screen

requirements.text: Extend the OrbitDock component (frontend/src/components/OrbitDock.tsx) to include a 4th dock item — Collections — alongside the existing Upload, Folders, and Mission items. The four items must be distributed symmetrically at 90° intervals on the circular orbit. The active (bottom-center) item remains the tap target; swipe gestures only rotate the orbit without opening any screen. All existing swipe behavior, infinite rotation, scale/opacity transitions, reduced-motion support, and orbital geometry must remain unchanged.

quotes: Το dock πλέον να περιλαμβάνει 4 εικονίδια: Upload, Folders, Mission, Collections
Να διατηρηθεί το ίδιο circular/orbital swipe behavior με infinite rotation

acceptanceCriteria: Dock renders exactly 4 icons: Upload, Folders, Mission, Collections
Icons are distributed at exactly 90° intervals on the orbit circle
Active icon always appears at the bottom-center position
Swiping left/right rotates the orbit and cycles items without opening any view
Tapping the center (active) icon opens the corresponding feature
No icon overlap at any rotation state
All existing scale/opacity/animation transitions are preserved
Reduced-motion mode works correctly with 4 items

requirements.text: Design and implement the Collections SVG icon inside OrbitDock with a professional, minimal 2×2 grid-of-squares style (thumbnail gallery symbolism). The icon must use rounded corners and a stroke width of 2.2–2.5px. Color: warm amber #D97706 in light mode and #FBBF24 in dark mode. When inactive, the icon renders at 60% opacity, matching the visual treatment of the other dock icons.

quotes: Τα ανενεργά να εμφανίζονται με 60% opacity

acceptanceCriteria: Collections icon is an inline SVG 2×2 grid of squares with rounded corners
Stroke width is between 2.2 and 2.5px
Icon color is #D97706 in light mode and #FBBF24 in dark mode
Inactive state renders at 60% opacity
Icon aesthetic is consistent with Upload, Folders, and Mission icons

requirements.text: Create a new CollectionsFullScreenView component (frontend/src/components/CollectionsFullScreenView.tsx) that opens when the Collections dock icon is tapped while active. This screen displays all uploaded items (files, notes, links) from the existing UploadContext and React Query file/note queries — the same data that already appears in the bottom-left floating stack — as an additional aggregated view. The existing bottom-left upload display must not change in any way.

quotes: Να ανοίγει νέα οθόνη με όλα τα αρχεία που έχουν γίνει upload
Τα uploads να συνεχίσουν να εμφανίζονται όπως ήδη εμφανίζονται στο κάτω αριστερό μέρος της οθόνης
Το Collections να είναι επιπλέον προβολή όλων των uploads συγκεντρωτικά

acceptanceCriteria: Tapping Collections icon when active opens CollectionsFullScreenView
Screen aggregates all uploaded files, notes, and links from existing data sources
Floating bottom-left upload stack is unchanged and still visible when Collections is closed
Screen has a back/close action to return to the main view
Component follows the same full-screen view pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView

requirements.text: In CollectionsFullScreenView, render all items in a responsive 4-column thumbnail grid. Each thumbnail cell is 80×80px on a 390px-wide mobile screen. Internal spacing between thumbnails is 8–12px. Screen-edge padding is 16px on left and right. Thumbnail corners are rounded 8–10px. Images and files render as visual previews. Notes render as card thumbnails with a note icon. Links render with a small favicon or a generic link-icon fallback. Implement lazy loading for large collections.

quotes: Lazy loading αν υπάρχουν πολλά αρχεία
Notes: Να εμφανίζονται με card thumbnail με icon note
Links: Να εμφανίζονται με μικρό preview thumbnail ή favicon fallback

acceptanceCriteria: Grid renders 4 columns at 390px screen width
Each cell is 80×80px with 8–12px gap between cells
16px padding on left and right screen edges
Thumbnail corners have 8–10px border-radius
Image/file items show a visual preview thumbnail
Note items show a card with a note/document icon
Link items show a favicon or fallback link icon
Lazy loading is applied so off-screen thumbnails load on scroll

requirements.text: In CollectionsFullScreenView, reuse the existing file-selection logic from the app (single select, multi-select, select all, move to Mission, move to Folder, delete, share) exactly as implemented in GallerySection and StackFilesFullScreenView. Do not create any new selection, mutation, or action logic — wire up the existing hooks, dialogs (SendToFolderDialog, MoveToMissionDialog), and mutation calls.

quotes: Να χρησιμοποιείται η ήδη υπάρχουσα λογική επιλογής αρχείων

acceptanceCriteria: Long-press or tap initiates single-item selection using existing selection state
Multi-select works the same as in GallerySection/StackFilesFullScreenView
Select All selects all items in the Collections grid
Move to Mission opens the existing MoveToMissionDialog
Move to Folder opens the existing SendToFolderDialog
Delete triggers the existing delete mutation/confirmation flow
Share triggers the existing share utility
No new mutation hooks or selection state managers are introduced

requirements.text: Wire CollectionsFullScreenView into HomePage.tsx (frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx) using the same conditional rendering pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView. Add a showCollections state flag and pass the onCollectionsTap callback to OrbitDock. No other existing state, routing, layout, or component behavior in HomePage must change.

quotes: Να μη μεταβληθεί καμία υπάρχουσα ροή
Να διατηρηθούν όλα τα υπάρχοντα animations
Να διατηρηθεί το professional, minimal ύφος

acceptanceCriteria: HomePage gains a showCollections boolean state, defaulting to false
OrbitDock receives an onCollectionsTap prop that sets showCollections to true
CollectionsFullScreenView mounts/unmounts based on showCollections
All other HomePage state and rendered components are unaffected
Existing Upload, Folders, and Mission tap callbacks are unchanged

constraints: Do not modify any existing functionality, logic, flow, animation, or architecture
Do not alter the floating bottom-left upload stack display
Do not create new selection, mutation, or file-action logic — reuse existing implementations
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve all existing OrbitDock animations and swipe behavior exactly
nonGoals: Changing how uploads are displayed in the bottom-left floating stack
Modifying the Upload, Folders, or Mission dock items or their screens
Adding backend schema changes for Collections (it reads existing upload data)
Creating a dedicated backend endpoint for Collections aggregation
Changing orbital radius, swipe velocity thresholds, or any carousel geometry beyond adding the 4th item at 90° distribution
